quick_facts.label: Award and academic level
quick_facts.value: B.Sc. is an undergraduate science degree. Its precise award title, subject combination, duration, credit pattern and exit options are determined by the university or college; UGC’s current undergraduate framework supports flexible, multidisciplinary, choice-based study and multiple entry/exit arrangements.

quick_facts.label: Typical entry qualification
quick_facts.value: Entry is normally after Class 12/equivalent, but required subjects and minimum marks are programme-specific. For example, Physics-focused and physical-science B.Sc. routes may require Physics and Mathematics, while life-science routes may require Biology/PCB; applicants must verify the chosen programme’s rules.

quick_facts.label: Admissions
quick_facts.value: CUET-UG is a major admission route for B.Sc. programmes at participating institutions, but a CUET score alone does not establish eligibility or admission: universities set their own programme requirements, subject combinations, counselling and selection rules.

quick_facts.label: Further study route
quick_facts.value: A B.Sc. can lead to M.Sc., M.Sc.-Ph.D., joint M.Sc.-Ph.D. and integrated Ph.D. pathways. IIT JAM is one national route; its 2026 admissions brochure states that candidates need an undergraduate degree or final-year status and must meet programme-specific minimum educational qualifications.

sections.heading: Eligibility: Class 12 science background, with subject requirements tied to the B.Sc. discipline
paragraphs: There is no single India-wide eligibility rule for every B.Sc. programme. The baseline is generally a recognised Class 12/equivalent qualification, but the required subjects, marks, age conditions where applicable, category relaxations and accepted boards are set by the admitting institution and can differ sharply by discipline.

For example, CUET programme listings show that Mathematics is central to many physical-science, mathematics and electronics combinations, while Biology is expected for programmes such as microbiology and biotechnology. Some applied B.Sc. programmes accept either PCM or PCB, whereas regulated or health-linked programmes may prescribe additional subjects, English or age requirements.

Before applying, match all three items—not just the course title: (1) the university’s Class 12 subject prerequisites, (2) the entrance-test subject combination if an entrance test is used, and (3) the applicable category/document requirements.
bullets: PCM is commonly relevant for Physics, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science and many physical-science programmes.
PCB is commonly relevant for Botany, Zoology, Microbiology, Biotechnology and other life-science programmes.
A science-stream background does not automatically make a candidate eligible for every B.Sc.; a university may require a particular subject studied and/or tested at Class 12 level.

sections.heading: Duration: distinguish a three-year bachelor’s degree from four-year honours pathways
paragraphs: In India, applicants should not treat “B.Sc.” as one uniform duration. Under the National Credit Framework, a three-year bachelor’s degree and a four-year bachelor’s degree with Honours/Honours with Research are distinct undergraduate outcomes. UGC’s Curriculum and Credit Framework for Undergraduate Programmes also provides for flexible credit-based, multidisciplinary study and multiple entry/exit arrangements.

Accordingly, confirm the exact award printed in the prospectus: B.Sc./B.Sc. Programme, B.Sc. (Hons.), or B.Sc. (Hons. with Research), as well as the number of semesters and the institution’s exit and re-entry rules. These details can affect later postgraduate eligibility and the depth of research preparation.
bullets: Three-year and four-year pathways should be compared by award title, major, credits, research component and the postgraduate programmes they support—not by the abbreviation “B.Sc.” alone.
Multiple entry/exit is a framework feature; its operational availability and conditions must be checked with the specific institution.

sections.heading: Curriculum: a science major plus practical, interdisciplinary and skill-oriented learning
paragraphs: The content of a B.Sc. is discipline-led rather than nationally identical. A Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Botany, Zoology, Computer Science, Statistics, Environmental Science or Biotechnology B.Sc. will have different core papers and laboratory/practical requirements. Under UGC’s CCFUP, undergraduate curricula are intended to use a flexible choice-based credit system and a multidisciplinary approach.

When comparing institutions, inspect the published semester-wise scheme for: core and elective papers, laboratory or fieldwork hours, mathematics/statistics/computing requirements, internship or research opportunities, assessment pattern and whether the final year includes a research project. These elements are more informative than a broad course label when planning for M.Sc. or technical roles.
bullets: Physical-science programmes commonly align their admission subject combinations around Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics.
Life-science programmes commonly align around Physics, Chemistry and Biology.
The exact syllabus, practical exposure and elective choices remain institution- and specialisation-specific.

sections.heading: Admission: use the institution’s route and choose entrance subjects strategically
paragraphs: B.Sc. admissions may be based on Class 12 merit, CUET-UG, or an institution-specific process. For CUET-UG participating institutions, the National Testing Agency conducts the examination, while participating universities retain responsibility for programme eligibility, subject mapping, counselling, merit preparation and final admission.

For the 2026 cycle, NTA published a CUET-UG information bulletin and application notices for undergraduate admission to participating central, state, deemed and private universities. Since admission calendars, seat matrices, subject requirements and counselling portals are institution-specific and change by cycle, applicants should treat the relevant university prospectus as the controlling document.

A practical application sequence is: shortlist the B.Sc. specialisation first; read its Class 12 prerequisites; identify the exact CUET subject combination or other test required; apply within the institution’s process; then complete counselling/document verification if selected.
bullets: Do not select CUET subjects solely from a generic science-stream list; select the combination required by each target B.Sc. programme.
A qualifying entrance score does not override a university’s subject or minimum-mark requirements.
Check official notices for the relevant admission year because dates, participating institutions and programme mappings vary.

sections.heading: After B.Sc.: postgraduate science, research-oriented study, teacher education and broader graduate pathways
paragraphs: For science specialisation and research preparation, common next steps include M.Sc., M.Sc. (Tech), joint M.Sc.-Ph.D., M.Sc.-Ph.D. dual-degree and integrated Ph.D. programmes. IIT JAM is a prominent route for several such programmes at IITs and associated institutions; however, each programme specifies minimum educational qualifications, including the undergraduate subjects that must have been studied.

A B.Sc. can also be used as the qualifying bachelor’s degree for professional or competitive pathways whose own eligibility rules are separately set—for example, teacher education, management admissions and public recruitment. Eligibility must be checked afresh for each destination; a B.Sc. title alone does not guarantee subject eligibility for a particular M.Sc., B.Ed., NET or recruitment post.

For B.Ed. planning, NCTE materials specify bachelor’s/master’s marks requirements under the applicable regulations, while states and institutions may administer admission through their own processes. Candidates should verify the currently applicable NCTE rules and the target institution’s notification before relying on an older regulation or draft proposal.
bullets: For M.Sc. or research pathways, preserve transcripts and verify prerequisite undergraduate subjects early.
For JAM-linked programmes, admission requires both a qualifying JAM result and satisfaction of the admitting institute’s programme-specific educational requirements.
For teaching, laboratory, clinical/paramedical and other applied roles, verify any additional professional-regulator, licensing, internship or employer requirements.
